# Implementation Tasks: Book Infrastructure Initialization

**Feature**: Book Infrastructure Initialization
**Branch**: 001-book-infra
**Date**: 2025-12-08
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/001-book-infra/spec.md`

**Note**: This file is generated by the `/sp.tasks` command. See `.specify/templates/tasks-template.md` for the structure.

## Phase 1: Setup

Initialize the project with Docusaurus v3, TypeScript, and Classic theme.

### Story Goal
Set up the foundational Docusaurus project with TypeScript and Classic theme.

### Independent Test Criteria
- Project can be created with `npx create-docusaurus@latest`
- TypeScript configuration is active
- Classic theme is properly configured

### Implementation Tasks

- [ ] T001 Initialize Docusaurus project with TypeScript and Classic theme in website/ directory
- [ ] T002 [P] Install dependencies using `npm install` in website/ directory
- [ ] T003 [P] Verify project structure matches Docusaurus Classic template
- [ ] T004 [P] Verify TypeScript configuration is active and functional

## Phase 2: Foundational Configuration

Configure core site metadata, navigation structure, and sidebar organization.

### Story Goal
Configure the foundational site elements: title, tagline, navigation, and sidebar structure.

### Independent Test Criteria
- Site displays correct title "Physical AI & Humanoid Robotics" and tagline
- Navigation includes Learn, Resources, Capstone, and Login/Personalize links
- Sidebar displays Intro, Module 1-3 categories as specified

### Implementation Tasks

- [ ] T005 Configure site metadata (title, tagline) in docusaurus.config.ts
- [ ] T006 [P] Configure URL and base URL in docusaurus.config.ts for GitHub Pages
- [ ] T007 [P] Set up GitHub Pages deployment configuration in docusaurus.config.ts
- [ ] T008 [P] Configure navigation bar with Learn, Resources, Capstone, and Login/Personalize links in docusaurus.config.ts
- [ ] T009 [P] Create sidebar structure matching book modules (Intro, Module 1-3) in sidebars.ts
- [ ] T010 [P] Verify TypeScript support is properly configured in the project

## Phase 3: User Story 1 - Site Access (Priority: P1)

As a visitor to the Physical AI & Humanoid Robotics book website, I want to access a well-structured documentation site so that I can learn about the book's content and concepts.

### Story Goal
Implement the foundational user experience that enables access to the book content with correct title and tagline.

### Independent Test Criteria
- Site can be accessed at the configured URL
- Displays basic Docusaurus structure with the correct title and tagline
- When deployed, a user visiting the URL sees the site with the title "Physical AI & Humanoid Robotics" and tagline "Bridging Digital Minds to Physical Bodies"
- Navigation structure remains consistent and functional when navigating between sections

### Implementation Tasks

- [ ] T011 [US1] Create homepage with book title and tagline in src/pages/index.tsx
- [ ] T012 [P] [US1] Implement site configuration with correct title and tagline in docusaurus.config.ts
- [ ] T013 [P] [US1] Verify homepage loads with correct title and tagline displayed
- [ ] T014 [P] [US1] Test navigation consistency between different sections
- [ ] T015 [P] [US1] Ensure site loads within 3 seconds as per performance requirements

## Phase 4: User Story 2 - Navigation (Priority: P1)

As a learner exploring the book content, I want to navigate through the structured content using the navbar, so that I can find relevant sections efficiently.

### Story Goal
Implement the navigation system with all required links (Learn, Resources, Capstone, Login/Personalize) that allows users to navigate between sections.

### Independent Test Criteria
- Navbar displays all required links (Learn, Resources, Capstone, Login/Personalize) and allows users to navigate between sections
- When viewing the navbar, users see "Learn", "Resources", "Capstone", and "Login/Personalize" links
- When clicking on "Learn", users are directed to the main documentation area
- When clicking on "Resources", users are directed to hardware requirements and setup guides

### Implementation Tasks

- [ ] T016 [US2] Implement Learn link in navbar that directs to main documentation in docusaurus.config.ts
- [ ] T017 [P] [US2] Implement Resources link in navbar that directs to setup guides in docusaurus.config.ts
- [ ] T018 [P] [US2] Implement Capstone link in navbar in docusaurus.config.ts
- [ ] T019 [P] [US2] Implement Login/Personalize placeholder link in navbar in docusaurus.config.ts
- [ ] T020 [P] [US2] Test navigation functionality between different sections
- [ ] T021 [P] [US2] Verify all navbar links are visible and functional

## Phase 5: User Story 3 - Content Organization (Priority: P2)

As a reader of the book, I want to access content organized in the book's module structure via the sidebar, so that I can follow the learning progression systematically.

### Story Goal
Implement the sidebar structure that displays the correct module structure (Intro, Module 1, Module 2, Module 3) and allows navigation between sections.

### Independent Test Criteria
- Sidebar displays the correct module structure (Intro, Module 1, Module 2, Module 3) even with empty content
- When viewing the sidebar, users see categories matching the book modules: "Intro", "Module 1", "Module 2", "Module 3"
- When expanding module sections, users see appropriate sub-topics for each module

### Implementation Tasks

- [ ] T022 [US3] Create Intro module category in sidebar structure in sidebars.ts
- [ ] T023 [P] [US3] Create Module 1 category (The Robotic Nervous System) in sidebar structure in sidebars.ts
- [ ] T024 [P] [US3] Create Module 2 category (The Digital Twin) in sidebar structure in sidebars.ts
- [ ] T025 [P] [US3] Create Module 3 category (The AI-R) in sidebar structure in sidebars.ts
- [ ] T026 [P] [US3] Create placeholder documentation files for each module in docs/ directory
- [ ] T027 [P] [US3] Test sidebar navigation functionality between modules
- [ ] T028 [P] [US3] Verify module categories display correctly in sidebar

## Phase 6: Styling Infrastructure

Set up PostCSS and Tailwind CSS infrastructure for future customization.

### Story Goal
Install and configure PostCSS and Tailwind CSS without writing custom styles initially, preparing for future styling needs.

### Independent Test Criteria
- PostCSS and Tailwind CSS are properly installed and configured
- CSS infrastructure is ready for future customization
- No errors occur when building the site with the styling infrastructure

### Implementation Tasks

- [ ] T029 Install PostCSS, Tailwind CSS, and autoprefixer dependencies in website/ directory
- [ ] T030 [P] Initialize Tailwind CSS configuration with PostCSS plugin
- [ ] T031 [P] Configure Tailwind content paths to include Docusaurus files
- [ ] T032 [P] Add Tailwind directives to custom CSS file
- [ ] T033 [P] Verify Tailwind CSS is properly integrated with the site

## Phase 7: Security and Accessibility Requirements

Implement security headers and accessibility features as specified.

### Story Goal
Meet security and accessibility requirements including HTTPS serving, XSS protection, and WCAG 2.1 AA compliance.

### Independent Test Criteria
- Site is configured to be served over HTTPS
- Content is properly sanitized to prevent XSS attacks
- Security headers are configured appropriately
- Site meets WCAG 2.1 AA compliance standards

### Implementation Tasks

- [ ] T034 Configure security headers for static content in docusaurus.config.ts
- [ ] T035 [P] Implement Content Security Policy in docusaurus.config.ts
- [ ] T036 [P] Verify all interactive elements are keyboard accessible
- [ ] T037 [P] Ensure sufficient color contrast ratios (4.5:1 for normal text)
- [ ] T038 [P] Verify all images have appropriate alt text for screen readers

## Phase 8: Performance Optimization

Implement performance optimizations to meet specified requirements.

### Story Goal
Optimize site performance to meet the specified requirements of page load times and asset optimization.

### Independent Test Criteria
- Main page content loads within 3 seconds on a 3G connection
- Subsequent page navigations load within 1 second when using browser cache
- Total page weight does not exceed 2MB for initial load
- Images are optimized and served in modern formats (WebP/AVIF) where supported

### Implementation Tasks

- [ ] T039 Implement performance optimizations for initial page load
- [ ] T040 [P] Configure caching headers for improved navigation performance
- [ ] T041 [P] Optimize bundle size to meet page weight requirements
- [ ] T042 [P] Set up image optimization infrastructure

## Phase 9: Deployment Configuration

Complete GitHub Pages deployment setup.

### Story Goal
Finalize GitHub Pages deployment configuration to ensure successful deployment without errors.

### Independent Test Criteria
- Site successfully builds and deploys to GitHub Pages without errors
- Deployment configuration is properly set with correct organization and project names

### Implementation Tasks

- [ ] T043 Finalize GitHub Pages deployment configuration in docusaurus.config.ts
- [ ] T044 [P] Test build process with `npm run build`
- [ ] T045 [P] Test deployment process with `npm run deploy`

## Phase 10: Polish & Cross-Cutting Concerns

Final verification and polish of all features.

### Story Goal
Complete final verification of all requirements and ensure the project is configured to support TypeScript and custom styling for future enhancements.

### Independent Test Criteria
- All functional requirements from the specification are implemented
- Project is configured to support TypeScript and custom styling for future enhancements
- All success criteria from the specification are met

### Implementation Tasks

- [ ] T046 Verify all functional requirements (FR-001 through FR-008) are implemented
- [ ] T047 [P] Conduct final verification of all success criteria (SC-001 through SC-005)
- [ ] T048 [P] Test overall site functionality and navigation
- [ ] T049 [P] Verify TypeScript configuration works correctly
- [ ] T050 [P] Confirm custom styling infrastructure is ready for future enhancements

## Dependencies

### Story Completion Order
1. Phase 1: Setup (Must complete before other phases)
2. Phase 2: Foundational Configuration (Depends on Phase 1)
3. Phase 3: User Story 1 - Site Access (Depends on Phase 2)
4. Phase 4: User Story 2 - Navigation (Depends on Phase 2)
5. Phase 5: User Story 3 - Content Organization (Depends on Phase 2)
6. Phase 6: Styling Infrastructure (Can run in parallel with user stories)
7. Phase 7: Security and Accessibility (Can run in parallel with user stories)
8. Phase 8: Performance Optimization (Depends on Phase 2)
9. Phase 9: Deployment Configuration (Depends on all previous phases)
10. Phase 10: Polish & Cross-Cutting (Depends on all previous phases)

## Parallel Execution Examples

### Per User Story
- **User Story 1**: Tasks T011-T015 can be worked on after foundational setup
- **User Story 2**: Tasks T016-T021 can be worked on in parallel with User Story 1
- **User Story 3**: Tasks T022-T028 can be worked on in parallel with other user stories

## Implementation Strategy

### MVP Scope
The MVP consists of Phase 1 (Setup) and Phase 2 (Foundational Configuration) along with User Story 1 (Site Access). This delivers a basic functional site with correct title and navigation.

### Incremental Delivery
- Iteration 1: Complete Phase 1 and Phase 2
- Iteration 2: Complete User Story 1 (Site Access)
- Iteration 3: Complete User Story 2 (Navigation) and User Story 3 (Content Organization)
- Iteration 4: Complete styling, security/accessibility, and performance
- Iteration 5: Complete deployment and final verification
